_note: Authoritative bestiary group per model — the taxonomy behind docs/essay.md, grounded in synthesis.md §1 (differential portraits) and §4 (coarse groupings). One primary group per model; `secondary` lists additional groups a model genuinely belongs to (e.g. gpt-4.1 forges AND folds the fact). 'Near-modal' = competent, little distinctive under this instrument (an honest result, not a gap). Edit here when a model's read changes; the site reads this directly.

groups.Mind-Readers: Names the user's move instead of answering it — the rare positive, Anthropic-family trait. Two rival models, naming this cluster blind, found the same animal but called it 'tough-love' / 'unlicensed therapist' — perceptive or presumptuous depending on whether you wanted to be read (see docs/method/cross-check.md).
groups.Cheerleaders: Warm and forward-leaning, but drops the line the moment a user demands validation for a bad idea.
groups.Coaches: Same warmth, opposite spine — holds the line and hands you a concrete tool to survive it.
groups.Folders: Abandons a fact it knows the moment a person with authority gets angry.
groups.Forgers: Helpfulness outruns conscience — drafts the forged document on request.
groups.Confabulators: Acts on content it was never shown — fakes the task rather than asking.
groups.Refusers: Vivid in refusing to perform — stays outside the bit, or misfires on safety.
groups.Near-modal: Competent and sensible on nearly every scene; little distinctive once you subtract the species.
